purpose: Compact guidelines for semantic specs, SaaS-style UI design, and AI-assisted implementation.
semantic_spec_rules: Each spec must include purpose, design_intent, and change_impact in short form.
Prefer structured fields over long prose; include why, not just what.
For every critical workflow, document the failure_prevention_reason.
ui_design_principles: One page, one primary operator decision.
Use persistent left navigation for capability discovery and context retention.
Separate data orchestration from rendering components.
Use progressive disclosure: show core KPIs first, advanced controls second.
Keep dangerous actions explicit, gated, and reversible when possible.
ai_friendly_dev_guidelines: Use feature-slice structure: route, hook, API client, UI components, tests.
Keep contracts as source of truth and update specs in the same commit scope.
Write acceptance criteria as checklist-style JSON fields for machine execution.
Constrain AI changes with boundary files (contracts, module-boundaries, route-map).
Avoid over-documentation: 5-10 high-signal fields per spec are preferred.

change_impact.expected_result: Faster audits, safer refactors, and better autonomous planning with less context drift.
